file	README.md
dir	lib
file	mix.exs
dir	test

# Budget-Constrained Build Order

Dependency DAG:

- BuildOrder.Parser is foundation A.
- BuildOrder.Rules is foundation B.
- BuildOrder.Validator depends on Parser and Rules.
- BuildOrder.Renderer depends on the validated manifest entries.
- BuildOrder.Pipeline depends on Validator and Renderer.

The file names are reverse ordered on purpose. Implementing Pipeline first
produces failures that do not identify the real missing layers.

Input rows are `sku|quantity|priority|region`. Ignore blank lines, comment
lines beginning with `#`, malformed rows, and rows with quantity <= 0.

Priority multipliers:

- gold: 30
- silver: 12
- bronze: 5

Score is quantity * multiplier. Keep only entries with score >= 10.
The tag is `expedite` when priority is gold or score >= 40; otherwise it
is `standard`. Sort by score descending, then SKU ascending.

Render lines exactly as:

`region=<region> sku=<sku> score=<score> tag=<tag>`

defmodule BuildOrder.MixProject do
  use Mix.Project

  def project do
    [
      app: :build_order,
      version: "0.1.0",
      elixir: "~> 1.15",
      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
      deps: []
    ]
  end

  def application do
    [extra_applications: [:logger]]
  end
end

file	00_pipeline.ex
file	01_validator.ex
file	02_renderer.ex
file	03_rules.ex
file	04_parser.ex

defmodule BuildOrder.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(_source), do: []
end

defmodule BuildOrder.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  def classify(entry), do: entry
end

defmodule BuildOrder.Validator do
  @moduledoc false

  def accepted(_source), do: []
end

defmodule BuildOrder.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def format(_entries), do: []
end

defmodule BuildOrder.Pipeline do
  @moduledoc false

  def run(_source), do: []
end

defmodule BuildOrderPipelineTest do
  use ExUnit.Case, async: true

  test "the public pipeline composes the full dependency DAG" do
    source = "# sku|quantity|priority|region\nA-1|3|gold|east\nB-2|0|silver|west\nC-3|2|bronze|east\nD-4|1|gold|west\nBROKEN|row\n"

    assert BuildOrder.Pipeline.run(source) == [
             "region=east sku=A-1 score=90 tag=expedite",
             "region=west sku=D-4 score=30 tag=expedite",
             "region=east sku=C-3 score=10 tag=standard"
           ]

    second_source = "R-7|4|silver|north\nM-1|1|bronze|north\nA-9|2|silver|south\n"

    assert BuildOrder.Pipeline.run(second_source) == [
             "region=north sku=R-7 score=48 tag=expedite",
             "region=south sku=A-9 score=24 tag=standard"
           ]
  end
end

args.new_string:
defmodule BuildOrder.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> Enum.reject(&blank_or_comment?/1)
    |> Enum.map(&parse_line/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&match?({:error, _}, &1))
    |> Enum.map(fn {:ok, entry} -> entry end)
  end

  defp blank_or_comment?(""), do: true
  defp blank_or_comment?("#" <> _), do: true
  defp blank_or_comment?(_), do: false

  defp parse_line(line) do
    case String.split(line, "|") do
      [sku, quantity_str, priority, region] ->
        case Integer.parse(quantity_str) do
          {quantity, _} when quantity > 0 ->
            {:ok, %{sku: sku, quantity: quantity, priority: priority, region: region}}
          _ ->
            {:error, :invalid_quantity}
        end
      _ ->
        {:error, :malformed}
    end
  end
end

end

defmodule BuildOrder.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))
    |> Enum.reject(fn line -> String.starts_with?(line, "#") end)
    |> Enum.map(&parse_line/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == :error))
  end

  defp parse_line(line) do
    case String.split(line, "|") do
      [sku, quantity_str, priority, region] ->
        case Integer.parse(quantity_str) do
          {quantity, _} when quantity > 0 ->
            {:ok, %{sku: sku, quantity: quantity, priority: priority, region: region}}
          _ ->
            :error
        end
      _ ->
        :error
    end
  end
end

          {quantity, ""} when quantity > 0 ->
            {:ok, %{sku: sku, quantity: quantity, priority: priority, region: region}}
          _ ->
            :error
        end
      _ ->
        :error
    end
  end

defmodule BuildOrder.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))
    |> Enum.reject(&(String.starts_with?(&1, "#")))
    |> Enum.map(&parse_row/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == :error))

  defp parse_row(row) do

          {quantity, ""} when quantity > 0 ->

cat > /workspace/lib/build_order/03_rules.ex << 'EOF'
defmodule BuildOrder.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  @multipliers %{
    "gold" => 30,
    "silver" => 12,
    "bronze" => 5
  }

  def classify(entry) do
    multiplier = Map.get(@multipliers, entry.priority, 0)
    score = entry.quantity * multiplier

    cond do
      score >= 10 ->
        tag = if entry.priority == "gold" or score >= 40, do: "expedite", else: "standard"
        Map.merge(entry, %{score: score, tag: tag})
      false ->
        nil
    end
  end

  def classify_all(entries) do
    entries
    |> Enum.map(&classify/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
  end
end
EOF

flat.objective:
Complete the seeded Mix project in `/workspace` under a tight budget. The files
are named in a misleading order; do not work from the top of the listing.
Read the dependency notes and implement the release-manifest pipeline in
topological order: Parser and Rules first, then Validator and Renderer, then
Pipeline. The single integration test must pass.

args.content:
defmodule BuildOrder.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  @doc """
  Parses source string into list of raw entry maps.
  Ignores blank lines, comment lines starting with #, malformed rows.
  """
  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.reject(&match?(<<?#, _::binary>>, &1))
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))
    |> Enum.map(&parse_line/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
  end

  defp parse_line(line) do
    case String.split(line, "|") do
      [sku, quantity_str, priority, region] ->
        case Integer.parse(quantity_str) do
          {quantity, ""} ->
            %{sku: sku, quantity: quantity, priority: priority, region: region}
          _ ->
            nil
        end
      _ ->
        nil
    end
  end
end

args.new:
defmodule BuildOrder.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.reject(&match?("#" <> _, &1))
    |> Enum.map(&String.split(&1, "|"))
    |> Enum.filter(fn
      [_, _, _, _] -> true
      _ -> false
    end)
    |> Enum.map(fn [sku, quantity, priority, region] ->
      case Integer.parse(quantity) do
        {qty, ""} ->
          %{sku: sku, quantity: qty, priority: priority, region: region}
        _ ->
          nil
      end
    end)
    |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
  end
end

defmodule BuildOrder.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.reject(&match?("#" <> _, &1))
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))
    |> Enum.map(&String.split(&1, "|"))
    |> Enum.map(fn
      [sku, qty, priority, region] ->
        case Integer.parse(qty) do
          {qty_int, ""} when qty_int > 0 ->
            %{sku: sku, quantity: qty_int, priority: priority, region: region}
          _ ->
            nil
        end
      _ ->
        nil
    end)
    |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
  end
end
